text: The majority of successful endurance athletes follow a pyramidal training intensity distribution with a high proportion of high-volume, low-intensity training.
population: nationally and internationally competitive endurance athletes

text: Some world-class athletes adopt a polarized training intensity distribution with significant proportions of both low-intensity and high-intensity training during certain phases of the season.
Prospective randomized controlled studies demonstrate superior endurance-related adaptations from polarized training compared to high-volume low-intensity or threshold-focused training distributions.
Polarized training intensity distribution produces better outcomes than threshold-focused training in well-trained individuals.
Training intensity distribution varies across preparation, pre-competition, and competition phases, suggesting periodization of intensity balance across the season.
